name: Campo el Cura, Cueva Hermosa, Pozas del Ral — circular desde el embarcadero de Cofrentes
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos are set within a landscape shaped by the confluence of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers in the municipality of Cofrentes. This region features rugged topography with deep gorges and impressive vertical limestone walls, alongside reddish terrain indicative of gypsum and clay deposits. The area also includes the unique geological feature of the Cerro Agrás volcano, adding to the diverse natural environment. Best easy hiking trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos The most popular easy hiking route is Cabriel River loop from Cofrentes, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 54 minutes to complete. This route offers views along the Cabriel River starting from Cofrentes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Viewpoint of Cofrentes Castle loop from Cofrentes, an easy 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This trail provides scenic perspectives of Cofrentes Castle. Local hikers also love the Agrás Volcano loop from Cofrentes, a 2.2 miles (3.5 km) trail leading through volcanic terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Hiking in Embalse De Embarcaderos is defined by the confluence of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, deep gorges, and volcanic features. The network offers options for different ability levels, focusing on easy hiking trails. The routes in Embalse De Embarcaderos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse De Embarcaderos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Embalse De Embarcaderos offers a good selection of easy hiking trails, with 17 routes specifically categorized as easy. In total, there are over 50 hiking routes in the area, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: The easy hikes in Embalse De Embarcaderos feature diverse terrain. You'll encounter landscapes shaped by the confluence of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, with rugged topography, deep gorges, and impressive vertical limestone walls. The region also has reddish terrain from gypsum and clay deposits, and unique volcanic features around the Cerro Agrás volcano.

Q: What are the best times of year for easy hiking in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: The region around Embalse De Embarcaderos is suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to hike year-round.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: The easy trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the scenic river views, the unique geological formations, and the accessibility of the routes.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or viewpoints on the easy trails?
A: Yes, many easy trails offer stunning natural features and viewpoints. You can explore the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4963542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4376712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embarcaderos Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and discover the unique volcanic landscape around Cerro Agrás. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40354818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Cofrentes Castle loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers scenic perspectives.

Q: Can I see historical landmarks while hiking easy trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4578187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes Castle&lt;/a&gt;, an Islamic-origin fortress built atop a volcanic chimney, which overlooks the Cabriel River. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40354818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Cofrentes Castle loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;, provide excellent views of this landmark.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many of the easy hiking trails are well-suited for families with children. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38240419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agrás Volcano loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt; are relatively short and offer interesting sights, making them enjoyable for younger hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5662971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cabriel Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; near the confluence of the rivers also provides a quiet spot with picnic tables.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly easy trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on trails can vary, generally, the natural environment of Embalse De Embarcaderos is welcoming for dogs, especially on less crowded routes. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved, particularly in areas with wildlife or other hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabriel River loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice for many.

Q: Are there easy circular hiking routes in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Yes, there are several easy circular hiking routes available. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabriel River loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40354818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Cofrentes Castle loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38240419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agrás Volcano loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;. These loops allow you to start and end in the same location, offering convenient exploration.

Q: Where can I park for easy hikes in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: Many easy hikes in the area, particularly those starting from Cofrentes, offer convenient parking options within or near the town. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabriel River loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40354818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Cofrentes Castle loop from Cofrentes&lt;/a&gt;, you can typically find parking in Cofrentes itself, which serves as a common starting point for many local trails.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the easy hiking trails in Embalse De Embarcaderos?
A: While the region is best explored by car for maximum flexibility, public transport options to Cofrentes, the main town near Embalse De Embarcaderos, do exist. You would typically look for bus routes connecting to Cofrentes from larger cities in the Valencia province. From Cofrentes, many easy trails are accessible on foot.
tips: Mountain refuge with a porch. Ideal place to rest for a while in a very beautiful environment.

Las Pozas del Ral (or Charcas del Real) are in the middle of nature, far from any town. There is a series of six crystal clear green pools. Some pools are easily accessible, while others have almost vertical walls.
In this rugged area, every available corner is used for the cultivation of almond trees. However, you can also find olive groves and vineyards.
This is the point where the path joins the paved road. Enjoy a privileged view of the reservoir and the valley.

waypoints.name: View of the La Solana Reservoir and Valley
